sentence: Chapter~3 established cross-hardware constraint vocabulary; this chapter narrows to NVIDIA Hopper and Blackwell---the reference GPU stack for cloud LLM decode in this book .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Production teams routinely over-invest in tensor-core kernel variants while decode remains bound by HBM traffic, launch orchestration, and shared-memory residency contracts .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: The design question on Hopper is not ``which op is fastest'' but ``which bytes cross register, shared memory, and global per token, and which async copy and sync primitives hide those crossings'' .
title: NVIDIA Hopper Architecture In-Depth

sentence: Every later CUDA MegaKernel chapter (6--11) and YiRage CUDA backend pass assumes the bounds derived here .
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

sentence: Decode megakernels on Hopper are won or lost in the memory hierarchy .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Global memory (HBM) stores weights, KV cache, and unavoidable spill traffic; decode at batch-1 revisits global footprints every token regardless of tensor-core nominal throughput .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Table~ tab:cuda_memory summarizes how each space participates in decoder fusion .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: The compiler and kernel author must co-design: promoting a value to registers speeds math but may drop occupancy; keeping too much in shared memory caps batch width within a CTA; spilling to global converts a fusion win into bandwidth defeat .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: YiRage CUDA lowering queries Hopper chip models for per-SM shared-memory budgets before accepting a fused stage .
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

sentence: Bank conflicts and padding rules still matter on Hopper: a fusion that looks correct on paper can serialize shared-memory ports and erase the expected speedup .
title: NVIDIA Hopper Architecture In-Depth

sentence: Chapter~6 generalizes lifetime planning across GPU/CPU/NPU; on CUDA the lifetime unit is the CTA tile bounded by shared memory and register files .
title: CUDA Graphs

sentence: Hopper's Tensor Memory Accelerator (TMA) is an asynchronous copy engine that moves multidimensional tiles from global memory into shared memory (and related tensor memory paths on newer generations) while MMA units work on previously prefetched data .
title: NVIDIA Hopper Architecture In-Depth

sentence: TMA decouples address calculation and bulk transfer from SIMT instruction issue---critical for decode fusion where scalar loads would fragment the instruction stream .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Chapter~8 formalizes TMA double-buffer scheduling; this section records the hardware prerequisite---without TMA-aware fusion, Hopper decode often behaves like an expensive Ampere port .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Hopper cluster launch allows a small set of CTAs in a cluster to share addressable DSM (distributed shared memory) and cluster-scope barriers .
title: NVIDIA Hopper Architecture In-Depth

sentence: DSM extends the fusion radius beyond a single block---useful when a decoder sub-stage needs modest cross-block reduction or halo exchange without round-tripping HBM .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Decode megakernels use clusters selectively---often for attention reductions or pipeline hand-off between fused stages---not as a default grid shape .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Compiler passes must annotate which edges require DSM versus shared memory versus global broadcast; YiRage records cluster eligibility in Hopper chip models to avoid illegal schedules .
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

sentence: CUDA decode fusion stacks four synchronization layers: warp-local shuffle, block barriers, cluster DSM fences, and grid-level events .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Over-syncing is a decode killer: each unnecessary grid-level barrier forces serialization across the entire launch .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Chapter~9 maps the same hierarchy across CPU and NPU; CUDA's warp primitive is the performance anchor competitors lack .
title: CUDA Graphs

sentence: Tensor cores accelerate MMA-shaped GEMMs and attention inner loops, but decode M-dimensions are often too thin to saturate tensor pipes at batch-1 .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Frameworks may still route decode matmuls through tensor-core libraries yet observe flat ms/token because epilogues scalarize and HBM dominates .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: The industrial rule used in this book: pursue tensor cores only when fusion keeps epilogues inside the same MMA stage and occupancy remains viable .
title: NVIDIA Tensor Core Evolution: From Volta To Blackwell

sentence: FlashAttention-class IO-aware attention is the positive example: tensor cores help when data movement is staged through shared memory rather than repeated global traversals .
title: NVIDIA Tensor Core Evolution: From Volta To Blackwell

sentence: For MLP slices in decode, manual megakernels sometimes accept scalar or narrow-SIMT paths when MMA setup overhead exceeds compute savings .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Compiler autotuning must treat ``tensor core enabled'' as a measured flag, not a default .
title: NVIDIA Tensor Core Evolution: From Volta To Blackwell

sentence: YiRage CUDA codegen emits PTX snippets only from vetted templates with simulator checks---not from ad-hoc agent output .
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

sentence: YiRage's CUDA backend maps dataflow IR to Hopper-legal schedules: shared-memory allocation, TMA descriptor setup, cluster eligibility, and sync placement .
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

sentence: CUDA Graphs capture integrates at this layer: once a MegaKernel schedule is legal and launch-static, graphs collapse host orchestration tax documented in Chapter~1 .
title: CUDA Graphs

sentence: Cross-hardware parity is explicit: a CUDA schedule that wins on HBM bytes may still be a poor template for XDNA in Chapter~5---the backend exports cost vectors for comparison, not a single ``best'' schedule .
title: CUDA Graphs

sentence: Fleet CI should compile golden decoder snippets per Hopper stepping and fail when shared-memory or TMA legality regresses .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Before shipping a Hopper decode change: enumerate Quantify bytes/token and launches/token with and without fusion .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Measure whether tensor-core paths improve ms/token or only improve profiler ``TC active'' counters .
title: NVIDIA Tensor Core Evolution: From Volta To Blackwell

sentence: Capture CUDA Graphs when launch count remains high after fusion .
title: CUDA Graphs

sentence: Chapter~3's constraint matrix gains numeric Hopper columns here; Chapter~5 translates the same fusion goals into XDNA static dataflow .
title: NVIDIA Hopper Architecture In-Depth

sentence: Readers should compare bytes/token improvements on Hopper against legality constraints on NPU---identical fusion boundaries are rarely optimal on both .
title: NVIDIA Hopper Architecture In-Depth

sentence: The CUDA deep dive is the reference GPU implementation; it is not the only target .
title: CUDA Graphs

sentence: Hopper decode optimization is bounded by register/shared/global residency, TMA async movement, selective cluster DSM, and minimal sync hierarchies .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: Tensor cores help decode only when fusion and occupancy align; PTX fast math supports attention epilogues .
title: Memory-Bound but Not Bandwidth-Limited: The Physical AI Inference Gap in Batch-1 LLM Decode

sentence: YiRage's CUDA backend encodes these bounds as legality checks and graph-capture-ready schedules .
title: YiRage: Multi-Backend LLM Inference Optimization

title: AMD XDNA Architecture
